{{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} [[File:源頼義.jpg|thumb|228px|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriyoshi'''|源 頼義||988 - August 27, 1075}} was a Japanese [[samurai]] lord who was the head of the [[Minamoto clan]] and served as ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]''. Along with his son [[Minamoto no Yoshiie]], he led the Imperial forces against rebellious forces in the north, a campaign called the [[Zenkunen War]], which would be followed some years later by the [[Gosannen War]].<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |pages=249–251}}</ref>
== Biography == His childhood name was Odaimaru (王代丸). He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North.
Yoriyoshi accompanied his father [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]]<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |pages=74–75, 83–85, 95–109}}</ref> on his own missions to defend the Empire, quelling rebellions and disturbances. Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the [[:en:Battle of Kawasaki|Battle of Kawasaki]].<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura]]<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the [[Kamakura shogunate]].
His son was [[Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who "would go on to be admired by his contemporaries as the greatest warrior."<ref name=Sato/>
In 1065, he ordained as a Buddhist monk and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shinkai''' (信海).
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]] * Mother: Shuri no Myobu * Wife: daughter of Taira no Naokata * Children: ** [[Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ** [[Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
==See also== * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

revision.text:
{{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} [[File:源頼義.jpg|thumb|228px|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriyoshi'''|源 頼義||988 - August 27, 1075}} was a Japanese [[samurai]] lord who was the head of the [[Minamoto clan]] and served as ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]''. Along with his son [[Minamoto no Yoshiie]], he led the Imperial forces against rebellious forces in the north, a campaign called the [[Zenkunen War]], which would be followed some years later by the [[:en:Gosannen War|Gosannen War]].<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |pages=249–251}}</ref>
== Biography == His childhood name was Odaimaru (王代丸). He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North.
Yoriyoshi accompanied his father [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]]<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |pages=74–75, 83–85, 95–109}}</ref> on his own missions to defend the Empire, quelling rebellions and disturbances. Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the [[:en:Battle of Kawasaki|Battle of Kawasaki]].<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura]]<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the [[Kamakura shogunate]].
His son was [[Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who "would go on to be admired by his contemporaries as the greatest warrior."<ref name=Sato/>
In 1065, he ordained as a Buddhist monk and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shinkai''' (信海).
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]] * Mother: Shuri no Myobu * Wife: daughter of Taira no Naokata * Children: ** [[Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ** [[Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
==See also== * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

{{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} [[File:源頼義.jpg|thumb|228px|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriyoshi'''|源 頼義||988 - August 27, 1075}} was a Japanese [[samurai]] lord who was the head of the [[Minamoto clan]] and served as ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]''. Along with his son [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], he led the Imperial forces against rebellious forces in the north, a campaign called the [[Zenkunen War]], which would be followed some years later by the [[:en:Gosannen War|Gosannen War]].<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |pages=249–251}}</ref>
== Biography == His childhood name was Odaimaru (王代丸). He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North.
Yoriyoshi accompanied his father [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]]<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |pages=74–75, 83–85, 95–109}}</ref> on his own missions to defend the Empire, quelling rebellions and disturbances. Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the [[:en:Battle of Kawasaki|Battle of Kawasaki]].<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]]<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]].
His son was [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who "would go on to be admired by his contemporaries as the greatest warrior."<ref name=Sato/>
In 1065, he ordained as a Buddhist monk and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shinkai''' (信海).
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]] * Mother: Shuri no Myobu * Wife: daughter of Taira no Naokata * Children: ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
==See also== * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.
{{Japan-mil-hist-stub}} {{japan-noble-stub}}

In 1065, he ordained as a Buddhist monk and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shinkai''' (信海).
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]] * Mother: Shuri no Myobu * Wife: daughter of Taira no Naokata * Children: ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
==See also== * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[cs:Jorijoši Minamoto]][[en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[es:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[fa:میناموتو نو یوری‌یوشی]][[fr:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ja:源頼義]][[pt:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёриёси]][[sv:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорійосі]][[zh:源賴義]] {{Infobox person|image=File:源頼義.jpg|birth_date=0988; {{birth date|0994|5|20}}|death_date={{death date|1075|8|27}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|military personnel|qid=Q47064}}|spouse={{ill|Taira no Naokata's daughter|qid=Q106418907}}|children={{ill|Kaiyo|qid=Q11491411}}; {{ill|Kawano Chikakiyo|qid=Q11554262}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|qid=Q3083118}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|qid=Q11564276}}; {{ill|Q106768766|qid=Q106768766}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Yorinobu|qid=Q2699951}}|native_name=源頼義}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3082851]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yoriyoshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} [[File:源頼義.jpg|thumb|228px|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriyoshi'''|源 頼義||988 - August 27, 1075}} was a Japanese [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] lord who was the head of the [[Minamoto clan]] and served as ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]''. Along with his son [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], he led the Imperial forces against rebellious forces in the north, a campaign called the [[:en:Zenkunen War|Zenkunen War]], which would be followed some years later by the [[:en:Gosannen War|Gosannen War]].<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |pages=249–251}}</ref>
== Biography == His childhood name was Odaimaru (王代丸). He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North.
Yoriyoshi accompanied his father [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]]<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |pages=74–75, 83–85, 95–109}}</ref> on his own missions to defend the Empire, quelling rebellions and disturbances. Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the [[:en:Battle of Kawasaki|Battle of Kawasaki]].<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]]<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]].
His son was [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who "would go on to be admired by his contemporaries as the greatest warrior."<ref name=Sato/>
In 1065, he ordained as a Buddhist monk and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shinkai''' (信海).
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]] * Mother: Shuri no Myobu * Wife: daughter of Taira no Naokata * Children: ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
==See also== * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.
{{Japan-mil-hist-stub}} {{japan-noble-stub}}

He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the [[:en:Battle of Kawasaki|Battle of Kawasaki]].<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]]<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]].

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[cs:Jorijoši Minamoto]][[en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[es:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[fa:میناموتو نو یوری‌یوشی]][[fr:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ja:源頼義]][[pt:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёриёси]][[sv:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорійосі]][[zh:源賴義]] {{Infobox person|image=File:源頼義.jpg|birth_date=0988; {{birth date|0994|5|20}}|death_date={{death date|1075|8|27}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|military personnel|WD=Q47064}}|spouse={{ill|Taira no Naokata's daughter|WD=Q106418907}}|children={{ill|Kaiyo|WD=Q11491411}}; {{ill|Kawano Chikakiyo|WD=Q11554262}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|WD=Q11564276}}; {{ill|Q106768766|WD=Q106768766}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Yorinobu|WD=Q2699951}}|native_name=源頼義}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3082851]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yoriyoshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} [[File:源頼義.jpg|thumb|228px|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriyoshi'''|源 頼義||988 - August 27, 1075}} was a Japanese [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] lord who was the head of the [[Minamoto clan]] and served as ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]''. Along with his son [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], he led the Imperial forces against rebellious forces in the north, a campaign called the [[:en:Zenkunen War|Zenkunen War]], which would be followed some years later by the [[:en:Gosannen War|Gosannen War]].<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |pages=249–251}}</ref>
== Biography == His childhood name was Odaimaru (王代丸). He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North.
Yoriyoshi accompanied his father [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]]<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |pages=74–75, 83–85, 95–109}}</ref> on his own missions to defend the Empire, quelling rebellions and disturbances. Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the [[:en:Battle of Kawasaki|Battle of Kawasaki]].<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]]<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]].
His son was [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who "would go on to be admired by his contemporaries as the greatest warrior."<ref name=Sato/>
In 1065, he ordained as a Buddhist monk and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shinkai''' (信海).
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]] * Mother: Shuri no Myobu * Wife: daughter of Taira no Naokata * Children: ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
==See also== * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the [[:en:Battle of Kawasaki|Battle of Kawasaki]].<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]]<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]].

Along with his son [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], he led the Imperial forces against rebellious forces in the north, a campaign called the [[:en:Zenkunen War|Zenkunen War]], which would be followed some years later by the [[:en:Gosannen War|Gosannen War]].<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |pages=249–251}}</ref>
== Biography == His childhood name was Odaimaru (王代丸). He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North.
Yoriyoshi accompanied his father [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]]<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |pages=74–75, 83–85, 95–109}}</ref> on his own missions to defend the Empire, quelling rebellions and disturbances. Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the [[:en:Battle of Kawasaki|Battle of Kawasaki]].<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]]<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]].
His son was [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who "would go on to be admired by his contemporaries as the greatest warrior."<ref name=Sato/>
In 1065, he ordained as a Buddhist monk and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shinkai''' (信海).
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]] * Mother: Shuri no Myobu * Wife: daughter of Taira no Naokata * Children: ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
==See also== * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[cs:Jorijoši Minamoto]][[en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[es:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[fa:میناموتو نو یوری‌یوشی]][[fr:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ja:源頼義]][[pt:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёриёси]][[sv:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорійосі]][[zh:源賴義]] {{Infobox person|image=File:源頼義.jpg|birth_date=0988; {{birth date|0994|5|20}}|death_date={{death date|1075|8|27}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|military personnel|WD=Q47064}}|spouse={{ill|Taira no Naokata's daughter|WD=Q106418907}}|children={{ill|Kaiyo|WD=Q11491411}}; {{ill|Kawano Chikakiyo|WD=Q11554262}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|WD=Q11564276}}; {{ill|Q106768766|WD=Q106768766}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Yorinobu|WD=Q2699951}}|native_name=源頼義}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3082851]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yoriyoshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} [[File:源頼義.jpg|thumb|228px|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriyoshi'''|源 頼義||988 - August 27, 1075}} was a Japanese [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] lord who was the head of the [[Minamoto clan]] and served as ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]''. Along with his son [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], he led the Imperial forces against rebellious forces in the north, a campaign called the [[:en:Zenkunen War|Zenkunen War]], which would be followed some years later by the [[:en:Gosannen War|Gosannen War]].<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |pages=249–251}}</ref>
== Biography == His childhood name was Odaimaru (王代丸). He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North.
Yoriyoshi accompanied his father [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]]<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |pages=74–75, 83–85, 95–109}}</ref> on his own missions to defend the Empire, quelling rebellions and disturbances. Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the [[:en:Battle of Kawasaki|Battle of Kawasaki]].<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]]<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]].
His son was [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who "would go on to be admired by his contemporaries as the greatest warrior."<ref name=Sato/>
In 1065, he ordained as a Buddhist monk and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shinkai''' (信海).
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]] * Mother: Shuri no Myobu * Wife: daughter of Taira no Naokata * Children: ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
==See also== * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[cs:Jorijoši Minamoto]][[en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[es:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[fa:میناموتو نو یوری‌یوشی]][[fr:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ja:源頼義]][[pt:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёриёси]][[sv:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорійосі]][[zh:源賴義]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[cs:Jorijoši Minamoto]][[en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[es:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[fa:میناموتو نو یوری‌یوشی]][[fr:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ja:源頼義]][[pt:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёриёси]][[sv:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорійосі]][[zh:源賴義]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[cs:Jorijoši Minamoto]][[en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[es:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[fa:میناموتو نو یوری‌یوشی]][[fr:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ja:源頼義]][[pt:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёриёси]][[sv:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорійосі]][[zh:源賴義]] {{Infobox person|image=File:源頼義.jpg|birth_date=0988; {{birth date|0994|5|20}}|death_date={{death date|1075|8|27}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|military personnel|WD=Q47064}}|spouse={{ill|Taira no Naokata's daughter|WD=Q106418907}}|children={{ill|Kaiyo|WD=Q11491411}}; {{ill|Kawano Chikakiyo|WD=Q11554262}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|WD=Q11564276}}; {{ill|Q106768766|WD=Q106768766}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Yorinobu|WD=Q2699951}}|native_name=源頼義}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3082851]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yoriyoshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} [[File:源頼義.jpg|thumb|228px|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriyoshi'''|源 頼義||988 - August 27, 1075}} was a Japanese [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] lord who was the head of the [[Minamoto clan]] and served as ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]''. Along with his son [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], he led the Imperial forces against rebellious forces in the north, a campaign called the [[:en:Zenkunen War|Zenkunen War]], which would be followed some years later by the [[:en:Gosannen War|Gosannen War]].<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |pages=249–251}}</ref>
== Biography == His childhood name was Odaimaru (王代丸). He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North.
Yoriyoshi accompanied his father [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]]<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |pages=74–75, 83–85, 95–109}}</ref> on his own missions to defend the Empire, quelling rebellions and disturbances. Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the [[:en:Battle of Kawasaki|Battle of Kawasaki]].<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]]<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]].
His son was [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who "would go on to be admired by his contemporaries as the greatest warrior."<ref name=Sato/>
In 1065, he ordained as a Buddhist monk and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shinkai''' (信海).
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]] * Mother: Shuri no Myobu * Wife: daughter of Taira no Naokata * Children: ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
==See also== * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

Yoriyoshi accompanied his father [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]]<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |pages=74–75, 83–85, 95–109}}</ref> on his own missions to defend the Empire, quelling rebellions and disturbances. Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the [[:en:Battle of Kawasaki|Battle of Kawasaki]].<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]]<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]].
His son was [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who "would go on to be admired by his contemporaries as the greatest warrior."<ref name=Sato/>
In 1065, he ordained as a Buddhist monk and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Shinkai''' (信海).
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]] * Mother: Shuri no Myobu * Wife: daughter of Taira no Naokata * Children: ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna]] ** [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]
==See also== * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[cs:Jorijoši Minamoto]][[en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[es:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[fa:میناموتو نو یوری‌یوشی]][[fr:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ja:源頼義]][[pt:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёриёси]][[sv:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорійосі]][[zh:源賴義]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[cs:Jorijoši Minamoto]][[en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[es:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[fa:میناموتو نو یوری‌یوشی]][[fr:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ja:源頼義]][[pt:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёриёси]][[sv:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорійосі]][[zh:源賴義]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[cs:Jorijoši Minamoto]][[en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[es:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[fa:میناموتو نو یوری‌یوشی]][[fr:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ja:源頼義]][[pt:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[ru:Минамото-но Ёриёси]][[sv:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]][[uk:Мінамото но Йорійосі]][[zh:源賴義]] {{Infobox person|image=File:源頼義.jpg|birth_date=0988; {{birth date|0994|5|20}}|death_date={{death date|1075|8|27}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|military personnel|WD=Q47064}}|spouse={{ill|Taira no Naokata's daughter|WD=Q106418907}}|children={{ill|Kaiyo|WD=Q11491411}}; {{ill|Kawano Chikakiyo|WD=Q11554262}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|WD=Q11564276}}; {{ill|Q106768766|WD=Q106768766}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Yorinobu|WD=Q2699951}}|native_name=源頼義}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3082851]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yoriyoshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} [[File:源頼義.jpg|thumb|228px|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriyoshi'''|源 頼義||988 - August 27, 1075}} was a Japanese {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}} lord who was the head of the [[Minamoto clan]] and served as ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]''. Along with his son {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, he led the Imperial forces against rebellious forces in the north, a campaign called the [[:en:Zenkunen War|Zenkunen War]], which would be followed some years later by the {{ill|Gosannen War|en|Gosannen War|WD=Q1187411|lt=Gosannen War}}.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |pages=249–251}}</ref>
== Biography == His childhood name was Odaimaru (王代丸). He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North.
Yoriyoshi accompanied his father [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]]<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |pages=74–75, 83–85, 95–109}}</ref> on his own missions to defend the Empire, quelling rebellions and disturbances. Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the {{ill|Battle of Kawasaki|en|Battle of Kawasaki|WD=Q3616452|lt=Battle of Kawasaki}}.<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura}}<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate}}.
His son was {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, who "would go on to be admired by his contemporaries as the greatest warrior."<ref name=Sato/>
In 1065, he ordained as a Buddhist monk and received the {{ill|Dharma name|en|Dharma name|WD=Q1543672|lt=Dharma name}} '''Shinkai''' (信海).
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]] * Mother: Shuri no Myobu * Wife: daughter of Taira no Naokata * Children: ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|WD=Q11564276|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}
==See also== * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

Along with his son {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, he led the Imperial forces against rebellious forces in the north, a campaign called the [[:en:Zenkunen War|Zenkunen War]], which would be followed some years later by the {{ill|Gosannen War|en|Gosannen War|WD=Q1187411|lt=Gosannen War}}.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |pages=249–251}}</ref>

Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the {{ill|Battle of Kawasaki|en|Battle of Kawasaki|WD=Q3616452|lt=Battle of Kawasaki}}.<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura}}<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate}}.

Along with his son {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, he led the Imperial forces against rebellious forces in the north, a campaign called the [[:en:Zenkunen War|Zenkunen War]], which would be followed some years later by the {{ill|Gosannen War|en|Gosannen War|WD=Q1187411|lt=Gosannen War}}.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |pages=249–251}}</ref>
== Biography == His childhood name was Odaimaru (王代丸). He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North.
Yoriyoshi accompanied his father [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]]<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |pages=74–75, 83–85, 95–109}}</ref> on his own missions to defend the Empire, quelling rebellions and disturbances. Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the {{ill|Battle of Kawasaki|en|Battle of Kawasaki|WD=Q3616452|lt=Battle of Kawasaki}}.<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura}}<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate}}.
His son was {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, who "would go on to be admired by his contemporaries as the greatest warrior."<ref name=Sato/>
In 1065, he ordained as a Buddhist monk and received the {{ill|Dharma name|en|Dharma name|WD=Q1543672|lt=Dharma name}} '''Shinkai''' (信海).
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]] * Mother: Shuri no Myobu * Wife: daughter of Taira no Naokata * Children: ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|WD=Q11564276|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}
==See also== * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the {{ill|Battle of Kawasaki|en|Battle of Kawasaki|WD=Q3616452|lt=Battle of Kawasaki}}.<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura}}<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate}}.

{{ill|Q106768766|WD=Q106768766|1=Daughter of Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, wife of Taira no Masazane}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Yorinobu|WD=Q2699951|1=Minamoto no Yorinobu}}|native_name=源頼義}} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} [[File:源頼義.jpg|thumb|228px|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriyoshi'''|源 頼義||988 - August 27, 1075}} was a Japanese {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} lord who was the head of the [[Minamoto clan]] and served as ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]''. Along with his son {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, he led the Imperial forces against rebellious forces in the north, a campaign called the [[:en:Zenkunen War|Zenkunen War]], which would be followed some years later by the {{ill|Gosannen War|en|Gosannen War|WD=Q1187411|lt=Gosannen War|1=Gosannen War}}.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |pages=249–251}}</ref>
== Biography == His childhood name was Odaimaru (王代丸). He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North.
Yoriyoshi accompanied his father [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]]<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |pages=74–75, 83–85, 95–109}}</ref> on his own missions to defend the Empire, quelling rebellions and disturbances. Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the {{ill|Battle of Kawasaki|en|Battle of Kawasaki|WD=Q3616452|lt=Battle of Kawasaki|1=Battle of Kawasaki}}.<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}}<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}.
His son was {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, who "would go on to be admired by his contemporaries as the greatest warrior."<ref name=Sato/>
In 1065, he ordained as a Buddhist monk and received the {{ill|Dharma name|en|Dharma name|WD=Q1543672|lt=Dharma name|1=Dharma name}} '''Shinkai''' (信海).
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]] * Mother: Shuri no Myobu * Wife: daughter of Taira no Naokata * Children: ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|WD=Q11564276|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|1=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}
==See also== * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the {{ill|Battle of Kawasaki|en|Battle of Kawasaki|qid=Q3616452|lt=Battle of Kawasaki|1=Battle of Kawasaki}}.<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}}<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|qid=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}.
His son was {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, who "would go on to be admired by his contemporaries as the greatest warrior."<ref name=Sato/>
In 1065, he ordained as a Buddhist monk and received the {{ill|Dharma name|en|Dharma name|qid=Q1543672|lt=Dharma name|1=Dharma name}} '''Shinkai''' (信海).
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]] * Mother: Shuri no Myobu * Wife: daughter of Taira no Naokata * Children: ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|qid=Q11564276|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|qid=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|1=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}
==See also== * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

{{Infobox person|image=File:源頼義.jpg|birth_date=0988; {{birth date|0994|5|20}}|death_date={{death date|1075|8|27}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|military personnel|qid=Q47064|1=Military personnel}}|spouse={{ill|Taira no Naokata's daughter|qid=Q106418907|1=Taira no Naokata's daughter}}|children={{ill|Kaiyo|qid=Q11491411|1=Kaiyo}}; {{ill|Kawano Chikakiyo|qid=Q11554262|1=Kawano Chikakiyo}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|qid=Q3083118|1=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|qid=Q11564276|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}}; {{ill|Q106768766|qid=Q106768766|1=Daughter of Minamoto no Yoriyoshi, wife of Taira no Masazane}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Yorinobu|qid=Q2699951|1=Minamoto no Yorinobu}}|native_name=源頼義}} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} [[File:源頼義.jpg|thumb|228px|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriyoshi'''|源 頼義||988 - August 27, 1075}} was a Japanese {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|qid=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} lord who was the head of the [[Minamoto clan]] and served as ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]''. Along with his son {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, he led the Imperial forces against rebellious forces in the north, a campaign called the [[:en:Zenkunen War|Zenkunen War]], which would be followed some years later by the {{ill|Gosannen War|en|Gosannen War|qid=Q1187411|lt=Gosannen War|1=Gosannen War}}.<ref name=Sansom>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A history of Japan to 1334 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1958 |ISBN=0804705232 |pages=249–251}}</ref>
== Biography == His childhood name was Odaimaru (王代丸). He held the title, passed down from his father, of ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'', Commander-in-chief of the Defense of the North.
Yoriyoshi accompanied his father [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]]<ref name=Sato>{{Cite book |last=Sato |first=Hiroaki |title=Legends of the Samurai |publisher=Overlook Duckworth |year=1995 |ISBN=9781590207307 |pages=74–75, 83–85, 95–109}}</ref> on his own missions to defend the Empire, quelling rebellions and disturbances. Thus he gained much of his knowledge of tactics and strategy. He fought in the Zenkunen War for twelve years starting in 1051, including the {{ill|Battle of Kawasaki|en|Battle of Kawasaki|qid=Q3616452|lt=Battle of Kawasaki|1=Battle of Kawasaki}}.<ref name=Turnbull2>{{Cite book |last=Turnbull |first=Stephen |title=The Samurai Sourcebook |publisher=Cassell & Co. |year=1998 |ISBN=1854095234 |page=199}}</ref> In 1063, Yoriyoshi founded [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}}<ref>[http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html 鶴岡八幡宮] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090201102318/http://www.hachimangu.or.jp/about/lineage/index.html |date=2009-02-01 }} "History of Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine" (in Japanese) retrieved on February 4, 2009</ref> which was to become, roughly a century later, the primary shrine of the Minamoto clan when they began the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|qid=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}.
His son was {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, who "would go on to be admired by his contemporaries as the greatest warrior."<ref name=Sato/>
In 1065, he ordained as a Buddhist monk and received the {{ill|Dharma name|en|Dharma name|qid=Q1543672|lt=Dharma name|1=Dharma name}} '''Shinkai''' (信海).
==Family== * Father: ''[[:en:Chinjufu-shōgun|Chinjufu-shōgun]]'' [[Minamoto no Yorinobu]] * Mother: Shuri no Myobu * Wife: daughter of Taira no Naokata * Children: ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|qid=Q11564276|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|qid=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|1=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}
==See also== * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * [[Hachiman]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *Sansom, George (1958). 'A History of Japan to 1334'. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.
